title: How is AI reshaping Vietnam fintech in 2026? Six use cases with proven payback
excerpt: Six AI use cases now have live deployments inside Vietnam fintech: alt-data credit scoring, real-time fraud + AML, card/loan approval automation, eKYC, customer-service automation, and cross-sell. VIB cut credit-card approval to 15-30 minutes; eKYC costs drop 60-80% per onboarding.
lede: Vietnam's fintech operators have moved AI from pilot to production across six concrete use cases — each with at least one live Vietnam-market deployment cited in disclosure or press. Ordered by 12-month operator-side payback strength, they cover alt-data underwriting, real-time fraud, approval automation, eKYC, customer service, and next-best-product cross-sell. The wins are measurable, not theoretical.
body:
Each below has at least one live Vietnam-market deployment cited in disclosure or press. Ordered by 12-month operator-side payback strength.

Use case 01 — How is alt-data credit scoring unlocking thin-file lending?

Telco, e-commerce, transaction-graph signals feed ML scoring for under-banked applicants. Kredivo reports USD 4 bn in regional business financing on alt-data underwriting; MoMo deploys similar in Vietnam [Kredivo 2025]. Deployed · MoMo · Kredivo · Fundiin.

Use case 02 — What is real-time fraud + AML monitoring delivering?

ML detection on transaction streams — QR-fraud +60% YoY pressure [GBG 2025] makes legacy rule engines obsolete. SBV sandbox eligibility now de facto requires ML fraud-scoring evidence. Deployed · VIB · TPBank · Cake.

Use case 03 — How fast can AI cut credit-card approval times?

VIB + Trusting Social: 15-30 min credit-card approval [VIB 2025] vs 2-3 days incumbent baseline. Approval-time compression is the headline metric; downstream effect is funnel conversion +30-50%. Deployed · VIB · Cake · MoMo.

Use case 04 — How much does AI cut eKYC costs?

OCR + LLM-assisted ID verification, utility-bill matching, liveness checks. Sandbox-track eKYC explicitly named in Decree 94/2025 [SBV 2025]. Per-onboarding cost drops 60-80%; throughput rises 5-10x. Deployed · TPBank LiveBank · Timo.

Use case 05 — Where does customer-service automation actually pay back?

Voice + chat triage for high-volume product-query traffic. Lower-priority deployment — ROI exists but is modest. Best fit: bank-owned digital subsidiaries with concentrated query categories (balance, statement, password). Deployed · Sacombank · TCB · ACB.

Use case 06 — What is the cross-sell next-best-product opportunity?

Personalization on installed wallet base. Highest-leverage on MoMo and ZaloPay scale (30M+ users). Payback 6-12 months from incremental attach — but requires clean unified-customer-profile data, which most operators do not yet have. Pilot · MoMo · ZaloPay.

GBG 2025 = GBG APAC Fraud Trends Report 2025 · Kredivo 2025 = Kredivo regional financing disclosure 2025 · SBV 2025 = State Bank Decree 94/2025/ND-CP · VIB 2025 = Vietnam International Bank x Trusting Social press release Sep-2025 · Kira estimates = KIRA in-house analyst triangulation

Methodology note: This insight is excerpted from the KIRA Research report Vietnam fintech — e-wallet consolidation and super-app monetization (2026). For the AI budget sizing, deployment maturity matrix, and operator-by-operator scorecard, see the source report.

title: How large is Vietnam's fintech market in 2026, and where is it heading by 2031?
excerpt: Vietnam's fintech revenue pool sits at USD 4.3 bn in 2026 and grows to USD 8.9 bn by 2031 at 15% CAGR. Transaction value, revenue pool, and net contribution margin are three different numbers — and the press, investors, and operators each cite a different one.
lede: Vietnam's fintech industry is widely cited with figures ranging from USD 50 bn to USD 4 bn — both are right, they're measuring different things. Our revenue-pool view (operator topline after interchange) sizes at USD 4.3 bn in 2026, growing to USD 8.9 bn by 2031 [Kira estimates]. The wider transaction-value funnel is USD 50+ bn. The press cites the first; investors price the third.
body:
Multiple definitions in play. Our revenue-pool view (operator topline after interchange) sizes at USD 4.3 bn in 2026 growing to USD 8.9 bn by 2031 [Kira estimates]. The wider transaction-value funnel — what the public reads — is USD 50+ bn. Both numbers are right; they're measuring different things.

What are the three layers of Vietnam fintech sizing?

Transaction value (gross volume cleared) is the largest number: USD ~50 bn digital-payment value in 2026 [PwC 2021 + Kira estimates], reaching USD 60 bn+ by 2030. Revenue pool (operator topline after costs of clearing) is ~10% of that — USD 4.3 bn. Net contribution margin is ~25% of revenue. The press cites the first; investors price the third.

How is growth split between payments and credit?

Payments revenue grows ~12% CAGR through 2031 — volume up, take-rate down. Embedded credit + BNPL revenue grows ~27% CAGR off a smaller base [Kira estimates]. Lending overtakes payments in marginal-dollar contribution by 2029. Wealth/investing is the tail bet — high LTV but slow ramp.

Why do CAGR estimates diverge so widely?

Public sources cite 15-21% CAGR depending on definition. We anchor at 15% CAGR for the revenue pool, ~18% for transaction value. Higher-CAGR numbers usually count gross flow growth — which double-counts as super-apps intermediate the same retail wallet.

PwC 2021 = PwC Vietnam Payments 2025 Outlook · Kira estimates = KIRA in-house analyst triangulation across operator filings + SBV statistics + super-app parent reports

Methodology note: This insight is excerpted from the KIRA Research report Vietnam fintech — e-wallet consolidation and super-app monetization (2026). For the full sizing model, segment splits, and triangulation logic, see the source report.

title: Who are the top players in Vietnam fintech in 2026, and how concentrated is the market?
excerpt: Vietnam's e-wallet market is a triopoly — MoMo, ZaloPay, and VNPay hold ~88% combined share. HHI sits at ~3,400, well into highly concentrated territory. Super-apps Grab and Shopee sit one layer above, taking 30-40% of embedded-finance volume by revenue share.
lede: Vietnam's e-wallet market looks crowded on the surface but is structurally a triopoly. Top-3 wallets hold ~88% combined share [Kira estimates]; HHI for e-wallets sits at ~3,400 [Kira estimates] — firmly in highly concentrated territory. Super-apps (Grab, Shopee) sit one layer above as channel intermediaries, taking 30-40% of embedded-finance volume by revenue share.
body:
Top-3 wallets hold ~88% combined share [Kira estimates]; HHI for e-wallets sits at ~3,400 [Kira estimates] — firmly in highly concentrated territory. Super-apps (Grab, Shopee) sit one layer above as channel intermediaries, taking 30-40% of embedded-finance volume by revenue share.

What does the triopoly structure mean for new entrants?

An HHI of ~3,400 sits well above the 2,500 highly concentrated threshold. The remaining 12% share for sub-scale wallets is structurally unprofitable — sub-scale operators cannot fund the attach products that compensate for thin payment take-rate.

Moca's 2024 exit from the wallet market [VietData 2025] is the canary. Expect 2-3 more sub-scale exits or acquisitions by 2027.

Who leads — and what is each player's edge?

RANK 01 · MoMo (M_Service) — Backed by Warburg Pincus, Goodwater, Affirma. Share ~56%, 31M+ active users. Merchant breadth. 50,000+ partners. Strongest bill-pay flywheel.

RANK 02 · ZaloPay — VNG Corporation (HOSE: VNZ). Share ~17%, 21M users. Social graph. Zalo 100M+ MAU integration. Revenue +47% in 2025.

RANK 03 · VNPay — VNLife · bank-rails operator. Share ~15%, 40+ bank partners. Bank integration. QR rail across most retail bank apps.

RANK 04 · Cake by VPBank — VPBank (HOSE: VPB) digital bank. 6.2M customers, TPV 2025 USD 12.8 bn. Only profitable digital bank. VPBank funding cost advantage.

Key stats: Active wallets 2025 ~50-60M · Top-3 share ~88% · Sub-scale exits 2024-2026F: 2-4.

Cimigo 2024 = Cimigo Vietnam E-Wallet Consumer Survey 2024 · VietData 2025 = Vietdata Moca Exit Market Analysis 2025 · VNG AR 2026 = VNG Corporation Annual Report 2025 · VPBank 2025 = VPBank 9M-2025 Disclosure on Cake · Kira estimates = KIRA in-house analyst triangulation

Methodology note: This insight is excerpted from the KIRA Research report Vietnam fintech — e-wallet consolidation and super-app monetization (2026). For full player profiles, super-app channel dynamics, and the consolidation playbook, see the source report.
